title: <div>  •  Dice Ritual → Rolling a special set of dice to determine the scale of
content:
• Dice Ritual → Rolling a special set of dice to determine the scale of consequence.
• Burning or Destroying Something → A player sacrifices a written statement, item card, or note.
• Classwide Witnessing or Oath-Taking → The ritual requires verbal or written recognition from other students.

📜 Why This Works:
✔ Makes major events feel real and immersive.
✔ Adds a tactile, performative element that deepens engagement.
✔ Prevents players from treating fate manipulations as casual or throwaway events.

📌 FINAL LOCK-IN QUESTIONS BEFORE EXECUTING VERSION 9.0

1. Should The Class Be Aware of Who Performs a Fate Manipulation, or Should It Be Possible to Do in Secret?

✔ Ex: Can a fate shift happen without the class knowing who did it, or must it be publicly declared?

2. Should There Be a Limited Number of Archetype Purges, or Can Any Player Purge Themselves If They Pay the Price?
context: From document: RPG Character System (Fully Integrated into Weekly Modules) — chunk 535 (chunk 535/555)
